claims.claim: Malaysia's palm oil exports to China fell 39% in the first ten months of 2025
claims.source: MPOC, 'Malaysia Seeks to Regain Palm Oil Market Share in China', 28 November 2025, https://www.mpoc.org.my/malaysia-seeks-to-regain-palm-oil-market-share-in-china/
claims.correction: The claim is broadly supported by MPOC/NST/MPOB-derived reporting, but should say 'almost 39%' rather than a precise 39%. A conflicting syndicated report stated 'almost 29%', so the discrepancy should be disclosed.
claims.stale_check: For full-year 2025, Reuters later reported Malaysia's palm oil exports to China fell 35.7%, so the Jan-Oct figure should not be presented as the latest full-year number.

claims.claim: The collapse predates Trump-Xi
claims.source: MPOC, 28 November 2025; Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China, Wang Yi briefing, 15 May 2026
claims.correction: The timing is correct: the Jan-Oct 2025 fall occurred before the May 2026 Trump-Xi summit.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue for the sequence of dates.

claims.claim: Plantation Minister Johari told MPOC in November the cause was price, not the new summit headlines
claims.source: MPOC, 'Johari: Drop in Palm Oil Exports to China Due to Price, Not Geopolitics', 27 November 2025
claims.correction: Accurate as attribution to Johari. For stronger precision, say Johari attributed the decline primarily to price pressures, while related MPOC reporting also mentions logistics and competitiveness.
claims.stale_check: The attribution remains valid, but the analysis should distinguish Johari's November 2025 explanation from market conditions in May 2026.

claims.claim: Malaysian CPO at RM4,200 a tonne, USD120 above soybean oil
claims.source: MPOC, 27 November 2025; MPOC, 'Crude Palm Oil Prices Expected to Hit RM4,200 per Tonne'
claims.correction: MPOC verifies Johari's US$120 per tonne palm-soybean oil price gap statement. The RM4,200 figure appears to come from a separate MPOC price forecast and was not verified as the price quoted by Johari in the 27 November statement.
claims.stale_check: The US$120 premium is a 2025 price context. The analysis itself notes the spread later reversed, so present-tense price framing requires current market confirmation.

claims.claim: Malaysia's 2024 exports to China were 1.39 million tonnes, down 5.3% on 2023
claims.source: MPOB, 'Overview of the Malaysian Oil Palm Industry in 2024'; Reuters, 23 February 2025
claims.correction: The volume and percentage decline are supported by MPOB-derived reporting.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue for the 2024 baseline.

claims.claim: China's vegetable oil consumption is structurally weighted toward soybean oil (44%) and rapeseed oil (24%), with palm oil at 18%
claims.source: AOCS, 'China's evolving edible oils industry'; referenced peer-reviewed source: Jamet and Chaumet, OCL, 2016
claims.correction: The percentages are traceable to an AOCS article citing older research. The analysis should not present them as current without a newer China consumption dataset.
claims.stale_check: Potentially stale because the underlying mix appears to rely on 2016-era research.

claims.claim: When the palm-soy spread inverts, Chinese refiners shift mix
claims.source: MPOC, 27 November 2025; Reuters, 9 February 2026
claims.correction: The direction of substitution is well supported, but the analysis should not imply price is the only factor. Reuters also cited cheaper canola and soybean oil alternatives and lower-cost Indonesian palm oil.
claims.stale_check: The price spread can change quickly; current spread should be verified if the point is used in present tense.

claims.claim: China signed a US$10 billion oilseed deal with Brazil in November 2025
claims.source: COFCO official news page, 6 November 2025; Oilcn report citing COFCO WeChat, 11 November 2025; Forum Macao, 11 November 2025
claims.correction: A COFCO official page verifies import-contract activity at CIIE and counterparties including major traders, but the directly checked official page did not independently verify the full US$10 billion and nearly 20 million tonne details. Treat the amount and volume as reported, not primary-verified.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue found, but the primary-source chain is incomplete.

claims.claim: COFCO bought roughly 20 million tonnes of soybeans, soybean oil and palm oil from ADM, Bunge, Cargill and Louis Dreyfus at the Shanghai Import Expo
claims.source: COFCO official news page, 6 November 2025; Oilcn report citing COFCO WeChat, 11 November 2025
claims.correction: Use 'signed import agreements reported at nearly 20 million tonnes' rather than 'bought' unless the contract terms, delivery timing and enforceability are verified from COFCO's original detailed announcement.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue found.

claims.claim: That competing supply is already booked
claims.source: Oilcn report citing COFCO WeChat, 11 November 2025; Forum Macao, 11 November 2025
claims.correction: The analysis overstates certainty. Signed import agreements are not the same as confirmed delivered supply.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue found.

claims.claim: US tariff frictions cut US soybean exports to China by roughly 75% from the prior year
claims.source: USDA FAS China Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026; Reuters, 12 May 2026
claims.correction: I could not verify the exact 75% year-on-year figure from a directly checked primary source.
claims.stale_check: Potentially stale because China resumed some US soybean purchases after the October 2025 commitment.

claims.claim: USTR Greer told Bloomberg China would buy 'double-digit billions per year for three years'
claims.source: Bloomberg, 15 May 2026; DTN Progressive Farmer, 15 May 2026; Reuters, 15 May 2026
claims.correction: Accurate, but it should be clear this was an expectation or statement by USTR Greer, not a Chinese-published commitment with product-level quantities.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue for the 15 May 2026 statement.

claims.claim: Chinese FM Wang Yi said working teams are still negotiating the relevant details
claims.source: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China, Wang Yi briefing, 15 May 2026
claims.correction: Accurate. The Chinese readout said teams were in consultation over details and would lock in outcomes expeditiously.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue for the official readout.

claims.claim: China has not formally committed
claims.source: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China, 15 May 2026; Reuters, 16 May 2026
claims.correction: The safer claim is: China had not publicly specified product-level, volume-level or value-level details of the incremental May 2026 agricultural purchase arrangement. The phrase 'has not formally committed' is too broad because China acknowledged continuing prior consensuses and consultations.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue, but this could change quickly if a formal implementation document is released.

claims.claim: July soybean futures fell 36 cents a bushel that day
claims.source: DTN Progressive Farmer, 15 May 2026; Investing.com market report, 15 May 2026
claims.correction: The market move is supported by market reporting, but a CME settlement page would be a stronger primary market-data source.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue for the event-day market move.

claims.claim: Boeing announced an order for 200 aircraft, less than half the 500 figure Trump had floated before the summit; Boeing shares fell 4%
claims.source: Reuters, 15 May 2026; AP, 15 May 2026; Barron's, 15 May 2026
claims.correction: The 200-aircraft figure is verified. The '500 figure Trump had floated' was not directly verified from the checked primary/source chain; reports variously describe expectations around 500 or Trump mentioning potential figures. The share fall also varies by timing: Reuters reported around 2.6% in morning trading, while Barron's reported larger declines over the session.
claims.stale_check: Market-price movement depends on timestamp and should be tied to a precise trading window.

claims.claim: The October 2025 prior commitment of 25 million metric tonnes of US soybeans annually through 2028 remains on paper
claims.source: White House Fact Sheet, 1 November 2025; American Soybean Association, 2025
claims.correction: The 25 MMT annual purchase commitment for 2026, 2027 and 2028 is verified from the White House fact sheet. 'Remains on paper' is interpretive and should be tied to actual shipment or export-sales data.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue for the existence of the October/November 2025 commitment.

claims.claim: Zero new-crop export orders booked for the 2025/26 marketing year as of the summit date
claims.source: USDA FAS Export Sales to China, 5 December 2025; USDA FAS Export Sales to China, 8 January 2026; USDA FAS China Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026
claims.correction: USDA FAS reported multiple 2025/26 soybean sales to China after October 2025, and the 2026 China Oilseeds Annual stated that, as of 26 February 2026, China had purchased or shipped 10.8 MMT toward the 12 MMT commitment.
claims.stale_check: Not a stale issue; the claim was contradicted by USDA data available before the May 2026 summit.

claims.claim: The story is Malaysian palm oil losing China to soybean oil, Brazil and Indonesia, a process two years entrenched before the cameras showed up in Beijing
claims.source: MPOB 2024 overview; Reuters, 9 February 2026; MPOC, 28 November 2025
claims.correction: The broad direction is plausible and partly supported, but 'two years entrenched' overstates the 2024 baseline because 2024 exports to China were down 5.3%, while the sharper fall occurred in 2025.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue, but the framing should distinguish 2024 mild decline from 2025 sharp contraction.

claims.claim: Mainstream framing puts the Trump-Xi summit at the centre of any threat to Malaysian palm oil
claims.source: No comprehensive media-content analysis or representative sample was provided or verified.
claims.correction: This is a media-framing claim and needs evidence.
claims.stale_check: Potentially changeable as new coverage appears.

claims.claim: Demand from China collapsed before the summit, primarily on price
claims.source: MPOC, 27 November 2025; Reuters, 9 February 2026
claims.correction: The timing is verified and Johari publicly attributed the fall mainly to price. However, Reuters and MPOC-related reporting also cite cheaper substitute oils, Indonesian competition, logistics and broader demand factors.
claims.stale_check: The price relationship can change.

claims.claim: Indonesia is absorbing 15.6 billion litres of its own palm oil into the B40 biodiesel mandate this year, with B50 planned for late 2026
claims.source: USDA FAS Indonesia Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026; Reuters, 13 January 2026
claims.correction: The 15.65 million kilolitre figure is better described as Indonesia's 2026 B40 biodiesel allocation, not literally '15.6 billion litres of palm oil'. B50 timing was reported as second half 2026 and subject to prices.
claims.stale_check: Potentially stale because B50 timing has been reported as moving toward mid-2026 or second-half 2026.

claims.claim: Bursa CPO under RM4,450 a tonne on summit day
claims.source: MPOC Daily Palm Oil Prices, 14 May 2026; MPOB BEPI Daily CPO Price, 14 May 2026
claims.correction: Verified: MPOC/MPOB data showed around RM4,393 to RM4,393.50 on 14 May 2026.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue for the quoted date.

claims.claim: Total palm oil exports down 14.34% in April 2026
claims.source: MPOB monthly statistical release via The Edge Malaysia, 12 May 2026; The Star, 12 May 2026
claims.correction: Verified as a month-on-month decline in total Malaysian palm oil exports in April 2026.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue for the April 2026 monthly release.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Malaysian CPO at RM4,200 a tonne
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The checked MPOC Johari article verifies the US$120 price gap but not RM4,200 as part of Johari's November statement.
The timing supports that the decline predated the summit, and Johari attributed it to price. But the analysis turns an attributed explanation into a settled causal finding.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: COFCO bought roughly 20 million tonnes of soybeans, soybean oil and palm oil from ADM, Bunge, Cargill and Louis Dreyfus
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The exact 20 million tonne and US$10 billion figures were not directly verified from the checked COFCO official page.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: That competing supply is already booked
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Signed import agreements do not necessarily prove delivery, binding capacity lock-in or timing.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Oilcn report citing COFCO WeChat, 11 November 2025

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: US tariff frictions cut US soybean exports to China by roughly 75% from the prior year
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The exact 75% figure was not verified from a directly checked primary source.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: USDA FAS China Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: zero new-crop export orders booked for the 2025/26 marketing year as of the summit date
cardinal_sin_flags.why: This is contradicted by USDA FAS export-sales announcements showing 2025/26 sales and purchases before May 2026.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: USDA FAS Export Sales to China, 5 December 2025

cardinal_sin_flags.why: This is directionally true only for public product-level or value-level details of the May 2026 incremental arrangement. China's official readout acknowledged continued consensuses and ongoing consultations.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China, 15 May 2026

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: China's vegetable oil consumption is structurally weighted toward soybean oil (44%) and rapeseed oil (24%), with palm oil at 18%
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The percentages appear to rely on an older AOCS article citing 2016 research.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Indonesia is absorbing 15.6 billion litres of its own palm oil into the B40 biodiesel mandate this year
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The 15.65 million kilolitre figure refers to biodiesel allocation under B40, not necessarily a one-for-one volume of raw palm oil absorbed.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: USDA FAS Indonesia Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Boeing announced an order for 200 aircraft, less than half the 500 figure Trump had floated before the summit; Boeing shares fell 4%
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The 200-aircraft order is verified, but the checked sources do not cleanly verify that Trump personally floated 500 before the summit, and share-price declines vary by source and timestamp.

omitted_facts: Reuters reported on 9 February 2026 that China's palm oil demand was expected to decline because cheaper canola and soybean oil alternatives were rising. Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/chinas-palm-oil-demand-drop-cheaper-alternatives-rise-analysts-say-2026-02-09/
Reuters reported that Malaysia's palm oil exports to China fell 35.7% in the previous full year, which is a useful full-year update to the Jan-Oct 2025 'almost 39%' figure.

The Chinese official readout from Wang Yi did not publish product-specific agricultural purchase quantities or values. It said the two teams were still consulting over details and would lock in outcomes expeditiously.

The White House fact sheet on the prior US-China arrangement stated China would purchase at least 12 MMT of US soybeans in the final two months of 2025 and at least 25 MMT annually in 2026, 2027 and 2028.

USDA FAS export-sales notices show China booked US soybeans for delivery during the 2025/26 marketing year before the May 2026 summit, contradicting the analysis's 'zero new-crop export orders' statement.
Indonesia's B50 timing was not simply 'late 2026'; Reuters reported it was subject to crude oil and CPO price conditions, with a possible second-half 2026 launch.
source_assessment: The source base is broad but uneven. It includes Malaysian industry/government-linked sources such as MPOC and MPOB-derived releases, US official and quasi-official sources such as the White House and USDA FAS, Chinese official material from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, commodity-market reporting from Reuters/Bloomberg/DTN, and industry/academic material. However, several key details are not backed by directly checked primary documents: the full COFCO Brazil contract volume/value, the exact 75% US soybean export drop, and the current Chinese edible-oil consumption mix.
overall_assessment: The core narrative is directionally credible: Malaysia's China palm-oil weakness clearly predates the May 2026 Trump-Xi summit, Johari publicly attributed the 2025 decline mainly to price pressures, China had not publicly specified product-level details of the May 2026 agricultural-purchase arrangement, and April 2026 Malaysian palm-oil export data show weakness. However, the analysis contains several publication-risk problems: one clearly incorrect statement about zero MY 2025/26 US soybean orders, overconfident causal language around price, unverified or secondary-sourced COFCO contract details, stale or insufficiently dated Chinese consumption-mix figures, and imprecise Boeing and Indonesia biodiesel framing. It is usable after tightening, but not yet safe as a high-confidence fact-checkable article without corrections.
